hip_fix: No HIP equivalent exists. If the only need is a human-readable kernel name for logging/profiling, don't try to recover it from a `const void*` function pointer at runtime -- track the name yourself at the call site (e.g. a string literal passed into your own launch-wrapper), or use compiler-provided __func__/__PRETTY_FUNCTION__ inside the kernel body itself if the name is needed on the device side.
explanation: cudaFuncGetName (added CUDA 12.3) resolves a kernel function pointer to its name string. HIP has no reflection-style equivalent that maps a device function pointer back to a name.
source: HIPIFY docs/reference/tables/CUDA_Runtime_API_functions_supported_by_HIP.md, 'Execution Control' section -- row `cudaFuncGetName|12.3| | | | | | | | | | |` (blank HIP column = no equivalent), github.com/ROCm/HIPIFY, amd-develop branch, verified 2026-07-11.

hip_fix: HIP's hipGraph API has no conditional-node support (no hipGraphConditionalHandleCreate or equivalent). Restructure the graph to avoid runtime-conditional execution inside it: either pre-instantiate separate graphs for each branch and have host code pick which to launch, or fall back to ordinary stream-based launches (outside a graph) for the conditional portion of the workload.
explanation: cudaGraphConditionalHandleCreate (added CUDA 12.3) creates a handle for a conditional graph node (if/while-style branching evaluated at graph launch time). HIP's CUDA Graphs support (hipGraph) covers the core graph API (hipGraphCreate, hipGraphLaunch, stream capture, etc.) but not this newer conditional-execution extension.
source: HIPIFY docs/reference/tables/CUDA_Runtime_API_functions_supported_by_HIP.md, 'Graph Management' section -- row `cudaGraphConditionalHandleCreate|12.3| | | | | | | | | | |`, github.com/ROCm/HIPIFY, amd-develop branch, verified 2026-07-11.

hip_fix: No HIP/ROCm runtime-level equivalent. Write-ordering/visibility guarantees for RDMA transfers on ROCm need to come from the RDMA transport layer itself (e.g. the fabric/network library's own completion semantics -- UCX, libfabric) rather than a GPU-runtime flush call. Code relying on this for correctness in a multi-node pipeline needs its synchronization redesigned around the transport library's guarantees.
explanation: cudaDeviceFlushGPUDirectRDMAWrites (added CUDA 11.3; signature `__host__ cudaError_t cudaDeviceFlushGPUDirectRDMAWrites(cudaFlushGPUDirectRDMAWritesTarget target, cudaFlushGPUDirectRDMAWritesScope scope)`) blocks until remote GPUDirect RDMA writes are visible to a specified scope -- used in multi-node HPC/AI training pipelines. HIP has no matching call.
source: HIPIFY docs/reference/tables/CUDA_Runtime_API_functions_supported_by_HIP.md, 'Device Management' section -- row `cudaDeviceFlushGPUDirectRDMAWrites|11.3| | | | | | | | | | |`, github.com/ROCm/HIPIFY, amd-develop branch; signature cross-checked against NVIDIA's CUDA Runtime API docs, verified 2026-07-11.

hip_fix: No HIP equivalent exists -- remove the call. HIP has no persisting-L2 cache-line concept to reset in the first place: if the port also uses the related hipAccessPolicyWindow setup (HIP's partial equivalent of CUDA's access-policy-window feature) elsewhere, that configuration governs AMD's cache behavior on its own terms and does not require (or support) an explicit reset step. If correctness genuinely depends on this reset's exact semantics rather than just being defensive cleanup, that logic needs redesigning -- ROCm's cache subsystem doesn't expose the same persistence controls in reverse.
explanation: cudaCtxResetPersistingL2Cache (added CUDA 11.0/11.3, `__host__ cudaError_t cudaCtxResetPersistingL2Cache(void)`, no arguments) resets all persisting L2 cache lines to normal status. hipify-perl explicitly lists it as a known-unsupported HIP identifier and leaves it completely untranslated (prints a stderr warning only, still exits 0) -- confirmed by actually running the real hipify-perl script against seeds/repairDemo.cu. HIP's real public header has no equivalent function under any name, though HIP DOES model the related *setup* side of the same feature (hipAccessPolicyWindow, hipAccessPropertyPersisting, hipDeviceAttributePersistingL2CacheMaxSize all exist) -- which is exactly what makes this gap easy to miss.
source: bin/hipify-perl:5584 (inside %hash_HipOnlyUnsupportedFunctions, declared at bin/hipify-perl:3114), confirmed absent from %map_core (the real substitution table, bin/hipify-perl:10286-16314); warning-only / exit-0 behavior confirmed at bin/hipify-perl:18186-18190 (zero exit() calls tied to warnings anywhere in the script); ALSO independently listed in docs/reference/tables/CUDA_Runtime_API_functions_supported_by_HIP.md line 71 (`cudaCtxResetPersistingL2Cache|11.0| | | | | | | | | | |`). github.com/ROCm/HIPIFY, amd-develop branch. HIP-side absence confirmed by direct search of github.com/ROCm/HIP, develop branch, include/hip/hip_runtime_api.h (10,524 lines). Verified 2026-07-11 by downloading and actually executing the real hipify-perl script against seeds/repairDemo.cu: exit code 0, stderr warning 'unsupported HIP identifier: cudaCtxResetPersistingL2Cache', translated output leaves the call byte-for-byte unchanged while cudaMalloc/cudaDeviceSynchronize/cudaFree in the same file translated normally.

auto_fix.note: Deletes the line entirely (old_text includes its own trailing newline, new_text is empty) rather than commenting it out or substituting a HIP call, since HIP has no equivalent and the call is unnecessary -- see this pattern's explanation field.
